skill.name: code-review
skill.description: Review code for bugs, security vulnerabilities, performance issues, and best practices. Use this skill whenever the user asks for a code review, shares code and wants feedback, mentions "review this", "check my code", "what's wrong with this code", pastes a diff or PR, or asks about code quality. Also trigger when users share code snippets and ask general questions that would benefit from a thorough review, even if they don't explicitly say "review".
skill.summary: Provides comprehensive code reviews covering security, correctness, performance, and architecture.
skill.author: EricDuMingTong

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 465 pattern matches, but all are FALSE POSITIVES. The 'external_commands' findings (352) are markdown code blocks in documentation files (```xml, ```kotlin), not actual shell commands. The 'weak crypto' and 'path traversal' mentions are educational content in security checklists describing vulnerabilities to detect, not actual usage. The skill is a code review assistant that reads reference documentation to guide reviews - it contains no executable attack vectors.

content.user_title: Review code for bugs and security issues
content.value_statement: Get comprehensive code reviews that catch security vulnerabilities, performance problems, and architectural issues before they reach production. This skill analyzes your code systematically across multiple dimensions to provide actionable feedback.
actual_capabilities: Identifies security vulnerabilities including XSS, SQL injection, path traversal, and authentication flaws
Detects performance issues like N+1 queries, inefficient loops, and resource leaks
Reviews code correctness including error handling, boundary conditions, and null safety
Evaluates architectural patterns and SOLID principle adherence
Checks UI component consistency for Nothing OS Android applications
Generates structured review reports saved as markdown files
limitations: Cannot execute or test code - analysis is static only
May not detect runtime-specific issues without execution context
Review depth depends on code context provided (snippets vs full files)
Does not replace human security audits for critical systems

use_cases.title: Pull Request Review
use_cases.description: Review code diffs before merging to catch issues early in the development cycle

use_cases.title: Security Audit
use_cases.description: Systematic security review of authentication, data handling, and API endpoints

use_cases.title: Learning Code Quality
use_cases.description: Understand best practices and improve coding skills through detailed feedback

prompt_templates.title: Quick Code Check
prompt_templates.prompt:
Review this code for any issues:

```language
// paste your code here
```
prompt_templates.scenario: Use for quick feedback on small code snippets

prompt_templates.title: Security-Focused Review
prompt_templates.prompt:
Perform a security review of this code. Focus on injection vulnerabilities, authentication issues, and data exposure risks:

```language
// paste your code here
```
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when security is the primary concern

prompt_templates.title: Full PR Review
prompt_templates.prompt:
Review this diff for bugs, security issues, performance problems, and code quality. Provide prioritized recommendations:

```diff
// paste your diff here
```
prompt_templates.scenario: Use for comprehensive pull request reviews

prompt_templates.title: Architecture Review
prompt_templates.prompt:
Analyze this code for SOLID principle violations, coupling issues, and refactoring opportunities. Suggest specific improvements with code examples:

```language
// paste your code here
```
prompt_templates.scenario: Use for design and architecture feedback

output: Problem: User input concatenated directly into SQL query
Suggestion: Use parameterized queries with prepared statements
WARNING Performance: N+1 query pattern detected
Problem: Database query executed inside loop causes O(n) queries
Suggestion: Use eager loading or batch fetch with JOIN

best_practices: Provide full file context when possible for more accurate reviews
Specify the review focus (security, performance, readability) to prioritize feedback
Save review results to markdown files for team collaboration and tracking
anti_patterns: Do not ignore CRITICAL findings - they indicate real security or correctness issues
Avoid reviewing production code without understanding business context
Do not rely solely on automated reviews for security-critical systems

faq.question: What programming languages does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill can review any programming language. It adapts its analysis based on language-specific patterns and best practices.

faq.question: Can this skill detect all security vulnerabilities?
faq.answer: No tool can detect all vulnerabilities. This skill provides systematic static analysis but should complement, not replace, human security reviews and penetration testing.

faq.question: How do I save the review results?
faq.answer: The skill automatically saves reviews to markdown files. Use review-commitId.md when a commit ID is available, or code-review-result.md as the default filename.

faq.question: Can this review diffs and pull requests?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide the diff in unified format and the skill will focus on changed lines while considering their interaction with existing code.

faq.question: What is Nothing UI component checking?
faq.answer: For Android projects using Nothing OS, the skill checks if UI components follow NothingUISupport guidelines for consistent user experience.

faq.question: How do I calibrate the review for different code maturity levels?
faq.answer: Mention the code context (prototype, production, legacy). The skill adjusts expectations - prototypes get architecture suggestions, production code gets strict security checks.
